name: Webfleet (TomTom Telematics) Fleet API
what_it_does: Webfleet (formerly TomTom Telematics) REST API for European fleet management and vehicle telematics. Enables AI agents to manage real-time vehicle location and route tracking for fleet operations, handle driver behavior scoring and coaching for fleet safety management, access fuel consumption and efficiency analytics for fleet cost optimization, retrieve tachograph data and driving time compliance for European transport regulations, manage job dispatch and route planning for field service and delivery operations, handle vehicle maintenance alerts and service scheduling for fleet maintenance, access driver workflow and forms management for field operations, retrieve vehicle utilization and asset management data for fleet efficiency, manage ADAS (Advanced Driver Assistance Systems) event data for safety programs, and integrate Webfleet data with TMS, ERP, and field service management platforms.
use_cases: Tracking European fleets in real-time from dispatch agents
Monitoring tachograph compliance from EU transport agents
Managing driver jobs from field service agents
Analyzing fuel efficiency from fleet management agents
not_for: Heavy trucking with FMCSA ELD requirements without European context
Maritime or rail without road vehicle fleet focus
best_when: A European fleet operator or van fleet manager using Webfleet wants AI agents to automate vehicle tracking, tachograph compliance, driver job management, and fleet analytics.
avoid_when: EU TRANSPORT REGULATION RISK: Webfleet tachograph data automation must comply with EU Regulation (EC) No 561/2006 (driving time rules) and Regulation (EU) No 165/2014 (tachograph rules) — automated driving time analysis for compliance reporting requires tachograph data integrity validation. Driver behavior scoring automation must comply with GDPR and applicable employee monitoring laws (works council co-determination requirements in Germany and other EU countries). Webfleet was divested from TomTom (2019, acquired by Bridgestone); verify current branding and API.

auth.notes: Webfleet uses OAuth 2.0 and API key authentication. REST API documentation at webfleet.com/api. Webhooks for fleet events. REST API with JSON. Amsterdam, Netherlands HQ (Webfleet). Bridgestone acquisition (2019, from TomTom, $1B). TomTom Telematics rebranded to Webfleet (2019). 50,000+ fleet customers. 700,000+ vehicles. Strong in European commercial vehicle and van fleet management. Competes with Samsara, Geotab, and Verizon Connect for European fleet telematics.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — per-vehicle monthly subscription
pricing.paid_tiers: Monthly per-vehicle subscription; hardware purchase or lease
pricing.notes: Amsterdam. Webfleet (Bridgestone acquisition 2019 from TomTom). 50,000+ fleet customers. 700,000+ vehicles. Strong in European commercial vehicles. Competes with Samsara, Geotab, and Verizon Connect for European fleet.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; documented per integration tier

known_agent_gotchas: EU TRANSPORT REGULATION RISK: Tachograph compliance automation must validate EU 561/2006 driving time rules; automated compliance reporting without data integrity check creates transport regulation liability
GDPR employee monitoring — driver behavior data and location tracking are personal data under GDPR; automated processing requires DPA (Data Protection Authority) compliant legal basis

German works council co-determination — automated driver monitoring in Germany requires works council agreement; deploying agent-driven driver monitoring without co-determination creates German labor law violation
TomTom to Webfleet rebrand — legacy TomTom Telematics API references may exist in older documentation; use current Webfleet API documentation and base URLs
Tachograph data integrity — smart tachograph data (GNSS-based) vs. analog tachograph have different accuracy levels; automated compliance analysis must account for data source type
EU vs. UK post-Brexit — Webfleet covers both EU and GB fleet regulations; Brexit creates divergence in GB tachograph and working time rules; automated compliance must handle GB-specific rules
agent_readiness.security_notes: European fleet. GDPR, SOC2. OAuth2/API key. EU. Vehicle location and driver data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Fleet telematics. GDPR, SOC2. OAuth2. EU. Vehicle and driver data.
